name: GPT-4 Generated Minority-Class Science Responses for Scorer Training
statement: Fang, Lee, and Zhai used GPT-4 to generate additional student-like science responses for minority scoring classes, then fine-tuned DistilBERT scorers on the augmented and original datasets.
population_or_context: Four short open-ended science response tasks with imbalanced score labels.
quantitative_note: The study reports task-specific improvements, with the abstract noting that 20% to 40% augmented data was needed for stable automatic-scoring improvement.
evaluation_method: Accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 comparisons across augmentation levels and against additional authentic student-response augmentation.
sparsity_or_imbalance_motivation: Unbalanced student responses across scoring categories, especially minority classes.
synthetic_data_research_role: Training augmentation and benchmark comparison against extra authentic minority-class responses.
reported_metrics: Accuracy, precision, recall, F1; task-specific saturation points.
limitations: The authors note that generated responses may not fully capture the true minority-class distribution.
source_locator: Abstract; Methods sections 3.1-3.4; Table 1; Results section.
